title: Milk and dairy decision sheet
summary: Practical guidance for “Milk and dairy decision sheet” within “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”. It supports safe storage, temperature control, preparation, rationing, and spoilage decisions, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Milk and dairy decision sheet” within “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”. It supports safe storage, temperature control, preparation, rationing, and spoilage decisions, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Stop condition: Do not use unknown plants, contaminated water, untreated sewage, unidentified chemicals, or diseased material in food production. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Reduced fallback: If “Milk and dairy decision sheet” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a shelf-stable meal prepared with safe water and clean utensils.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Identify crop or animal needs, season, soil, water quality, exposure, labour, and the smallest useful trial. Record the result under “Food · Agriculture · Preservation” before continuing “Milk and dairy decision sheet”.

Keep clean stock, seed, feed, harvest, compost, manure, chemicals, and diseased material separated and labelled. Recheck this point before closing “Milk and dairy decision sheet” for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
Use observation rows or small test plots before changing the whole system. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Milk and dairy decision sheet” for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
Record weather, watering, inputs, pests, disease signs, growth, yield, losses, and labour. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Milk and dairy decision sheet”.

Save diverse healthy seed or breeding stock only from known sources and revise the next cycle from measured results. Include the outcome of “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in the handover for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
warnings: Do not use unknown plants, contaminated water, untreated sewage, unidentified chemicals, or diseased material in food production. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
Avoid changing every plot, seed lot, feed source, or animal group at once. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
common_mistakes: Skipping the opening control makes “Milk and dairy decision sheet” unreliable: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Treating the result as safe while ignoring this limit is a serious error: Do not use unknown plants, contaminated water, untreated sewage, unidentified chemicals, or diseased material in food production. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Failing to document the final handover action leaves the next person without a trustworthy starting point: Save diverse healthy seed or breeding stock only from known sources and revise the next cycle from measured results. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
alternatives: If “Milk and dairy decision sheet” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a shelf-stable meal prepared with safe water and clean utensils.
Use a dated paper note and spoken read-back for “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation” when the normal form, device, or communication channel is unavailable.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Milk and dairy decision sheet” when growth, yield, water use, soil condition, pest pressure, disease, or animal welfare worsens after the change. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Stop and reassess “Milk and dairy decision sheet” when records cannot distinguish the trial from the control or the source of seed, feed, water, and inputs becomes uncertain. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Milk and dairy decision sheet” while temperature history, allergen status, flood contact, packaging integrity, or the origin of the food cannot be established. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

This card is not authorization to taste questionable food to test it, hide spoilage with cooking, or serve an unknown ingredient to someone with a serious allergy. Topic-specific stop check for “Milk and dairy decision sheet”: also follow this warning: “Do not use unknown plants, contaminated water, untreated sewage, unidentified chemicals, or diseased material in food production in “Milk and dairy decision sheet”.”. Apply this boundary to “Milk and dairy decision sheet” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

long_term: Review “Milk and dairy decision sheet” after real use in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”; replace failed materials, practise the handover, and update thresholds when official guidance or local conditions change.
if_method_fails: If the method described in “Milk and dairy decision sheet” gives no verifiable result, stop relying on it, isolate the uncertain output, record where it failed, and switch to a shelf-stable meal prepared with safe water and clean utensils.
environment_notes: Adapt the guidance in “Milk and dairy decision sheet” for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation” to weather, lighting, crowding, accessibility, power, water, transport, and available skill without removing the stop condition or independent check.
